title: Software Engineer 5 - TV & Web Player Platform
description: At Netflix, our mission is to entertain the world. Together, we are writing the next episode - pushing the boundaries of storytelling, global fandom and making the unimaginable a reality. We are a dream team obsessed with the uncomfortable excitement of discovering what happens when you merge creativity, intuition and cutting-edge technology. Come be a part of what’s next. The TeamThe TV & Web Player Platform team is responsible for developing streaming video players that deliver industry-leading playback experiences within the Netflix app on consumer electronic devices and web browsers. Our players operate on millions of devices, including Smart TVs, streaming sticks, game consoles, set-top boxes, and browsers like Chrome, Firefox, and Edge. We aim to delight our members with innovative playback features and exceptional streaming quality.Our  team is at the forefront of testing a diverse set of innovative activities related to product feature expansion. This includes creating tests for playback features such as Live Streaming, Seamless Ads playback, and Ident innovation. We conduct A/B tests to thoroughly evaluate features and ensure they perform optimally without regressions. We are deeply involved in continuous integration and release management testing of our video player, which is core to our operations. This critical function ensures the delivery of the best streaming experience to our members by maintaining high standards of quality and performance. Our team collaborates with various cross-functional teams across Netflix, including UI, Encoding, Streaming Algorithms, Security, and Open Connect, to support product development and experimentation through rigorous testing.This  team consists of engineers from diverse backgrounds, bringing expertise across multiple disciplines, including embedded systems, streaming media playback, content protection, client-server communication, networking protocols, continuous integration, test automation, experimentation, and software engineering. Our success is driven by self-motivated, collaborative, and curious engineers who prioritize the team's goals.The RoleWe are seeking a senior software engineer with deep expertise in test development, automation, CI/CD pipelines, and build and release processes to join our team. Your primary responsibilities will include designing and implementing complex automated tests and strategies that ensure the highest quality streaming experiences on both TV and Web devices. You will lead efforts to optimize and automate our build, integration, and release workflows, ensuring rapid and reliable delivery of new features and fixes. You will drive technical direction for test automation, mentor other engineers, and partner with cross-functional teams to solve challenging problems at scale.What sets you apart?You take technical ownership of test development and automation, designing robust solutions that address complex and evolving challenges..You have hands-on experience improving and maintaining CI/CD pipelines, build systems, and release automation, ensuring high velocity and quality in software delivery.You proactively identify opportunities to improve streaming quality and reliability, using data and experimentation to drive continuous improvement.You communicate clearly and collaborate effectively across teams and disciplines to align on technical direction and deliver high-impact results.Demonstrated experience utilizing GenAI  to accelerate test automation and/or streamline CI/CD/release processes.What skills are essential to us?Extensive experience in software engineering with focus on  test developmentExceptional proficiency in JavaScript and/or Typescript.Strong experience with CI/CD tools (such as Jenkins, Spinnaker, or GitHub Actions), and a proven ability to automate and optimize build, integration, and release processes.Proven ability to drive quality through experimentation, including designing, running, and analyzing A/B tests to inform product and platform improvements.Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to identifying root causes and implementing effective solutions.Exceptional communication and collaboration skills, enabling you to work seamlessly across cross-functional teams and influence technical direction.Experience mentoring and leveling up peers through code reviews, technical guidance, and knowledge sharing.Nice to have: Experience in streaming media playback, architecture, and industry protocols relevant to video streaming.  Generally, our compensation structure consists solely of an annual salary; we do not have bonuses. title: Production Designer - Brand Creative Studio
description:
At Netflix, our mission is to entertain the world. Together, we are writing the next episode - pushing the boundaries of storytelling, global fandom and making the unimaginable a reality. We are a dream team obsessed with the uncomfortable excitement of discovering what happens when you merge creativity, intuition and cutting-edge technology. Come be a part of what’s next.Production Designer, Brand Creative StudioTHE TEAMThe Netflix Brand Creative Studio (BCS) is our in-house team of Strategists, Creatives, Designers, Producers, and Program Managers. We are the creative collective behind Netflix’s most high-profile brand initiatives, developing work that builds passionate fandoms and shapes how the world connects with our brand. Our mission is to strengthen the Netflix brand and drive business value by creating culturally relevant marketing that is as entertaining as our shows, films, games, and experiences. Our studio serves as a centralized partner for a wide range of business-critical areas—spanning Global Brand and Brand Partnerships, Ads, Consumer Products, and live experiences like Netflix House. As Netflix continues to expand into new business units, formats, and physical experiences, the volume and complexity of design output across the company continues to grow. To support this scale, BCS is evolving its operating model to include a dedicated production design team focused on speed, systems, and consistency, without compromising craft.THE ROLEWe are seeking a Production Designer to join the Brand Creative Studio. This role is an individual contributor within the production design team, responsible for delivering high-quality, brand-led design output at scale across multiple business functions, including the Netflix Brand, Netflix House, Product Marketing, Consumer Products, and Corporate Communications.This role reflects a creative agency and design studio model of production design, where designers work within established systems to execute a high volume of work efficiently and thoughtfully. You will partner closely with the Senior Manager, Production Design and cross-functional teams to bring brand strategy and design systems to life across channels, markets, and formats.A strong portfolio demonstrating solid design fundamentals, attention to detail, and experience working within brand systems is required.RESPONSIBILITIESProduction Design & ExecutionDesign and produce high-volume, brand-led assets across a range of outputs, including lower-funnel marketing assets, campaign toolkits, sales materials, and presentation design.
 Execute work within established brand systems, templates, and production frameworks, ensuring consistency and quality across deliverables.
 Apply strong design fundamentals to every output, with attention to typography, layout, hierarchy, and clarity.Cross-Functional Creative SupportCollaborate with partners across Brand, Product Marketing, Consumer Products, Netflix House, and Corporate Comms to support a wide range of creative needs.
 Contribute to presentation design for executive, sales, and high-priority communications where clarity and brand expression are critical.
 Ensure work is fit-for-purpose across audiences and channels while adhering to Netflix brand standards.Systems & Workflow ContributionSupport the ongoing development and refinement of templates, modular systems, and production workflows.
 Adapt existing systems to new formats, channels, and use cases as needed.
 Work efficiently within fast-moving timelines while maintaining a high standard of craft.Craft, Quality & GrowthUphold quality and attention to detail across all production outputs.
 Incorporate feedback thoughtfully and iterate quickly.
 Stay current on best practices in production design, brand systems, and design tooling.
 QUALIFICATIONS4–6 years of experience in design within a creative agency, design studio, or in-house brand team.
 Strong foundation in visual design, including typography, layout, hierarchy, and composition.
 Experience working within established brand systems and templates.
 Ability to execute high-volume design work efficiently without sacrificing quality.
 Exposure to a range of outputs, such as marketing assets, toolkits, sales materials, and presentation design.
 Strong collaboration skills and comfort working with cross-functional partners.
 Fluency in modern design tools and workflows (Figma, Adobe Creative Suite; motion experience a plus).
 Clear communicator who is receptive to feedback and eager to learn.
 Highly organized and comfortable working in fast-paced environments.
 Interest in growing within a production design or systems-led design practice. Generally, our compensation structure consists solely of an annual salary; we do not have bonuses. You will help shape how Netflix Playground evolves, from the native client experiences kids interact with to the application layer that makes those experiences fast, reliable, and safe.Our work involves large-scale A/B experiments, the WebView and JS bridge layer that enables web-based games to run inside native clients, and direct collaboration with game developers to define SDK and integration points. For this role, that means API design, service contracts, application logic, and observability at scale. You understand WebView and JS bridge integration. The technical challenges of running games inside a native container are interesting to you on their own terms.You are comfortable with greenfield work. Netflix Playground is still being built. AP processes ~ 300K invoices and disburses ~27B annually. We are seeking a Manager of Accounts Payable to lead our Americas AP team. maintaining appropriate quality thresholds.Partner with cross‑functional teams to identify and implement automation and assistive technology solutions that streamline localization workflows, support scale, and improve overall efficiency.Cross-Functional Leadership & CollaborationAct as the primary localization point of contact for the live and emerging media titles under your management, description: At Netflix, our mission is to entertain the world. The challenge ahead involves scaling our ad tech to maximize its impact on our business.Our TeamThe Ads Platform Engineering teams build advertising systems and integrations that power the delivery of ads using our world-class content delivery ecosystem. We use a number of Netflix investments and innovations to power our ads - unique mix of client and server-side ad insertions, state of the art content delivery system, ad encoding recipes, content understanding and metadata. We deliver ads in a manner that’s thoughtful of our member’s viewing experience and drive great outcomes for advertisers. We also ensure that advertiser brand safety is ensured during serving, members only see the most appropriate ads for them.Ads policies are the foundational guidelines that govern what ads can be shown, where they can run, and under what conditions on the Netflix platform. Policies ensure that every ad campaign complies with both local regulations and Netflix standards, by defining rules related to restricted categories, maturity levels, and brand restrictions. The Ads Policies team is responsible for building the platform-level components required for managing Netflix policies and ensuring their enforcement across the entire ads ecosystem, including creative review, campaign setup, targeting, serving and reporting.This role is part of the company's strategic initiative to build a world-class advertising ecosystem following the launch of their ad-supported tier. description:

The Content Production & Studio (CPS) Product team is responsible for driving the strategy, prioritization, and execution of Netflix’s technology investments focused on creating and managing the production of content. We work with diverse and globally-distributed groups of business users, productions, and vendors. Our team’s primary superpower is the ability to work creatively and collaboratively in highly technical environments. As a member of this team, you will lead the integration of Netflix’s flagship generative AI capabilities into a unified, intuitive artist studio experience.The Role 
 We are seeking a Product Manager with deep expertise at the intersection of generative AI and creative tooling. You will define the product vision and end-to-end workflows for an AI-powered Creative Suite, leveraging state-of-the-art generative image and video capabilities into a unified surface that amplifies human creativity. Leading a team focused on innovation, you will collaborate closely with AI research and engineering, filmmakers, user researchers, and cross-functional partners to design creator-centric features that compound creative possibilities. As creative tools undergo a transformational leap, you will set new standards for AI-assisted production and editorial workflows, user experience, and technology, while keeping the artist at the center.This role requires a unique combination of strong product management expertise, a firm grasp of generative image and video capabilities, and a deep understanding of the creative tool ecosystem. You will address the complex needs of creatives (including directors, editors, colorists, and VFX artists), using cutting-edge technologies to shape the future of cinematic storytelling.This is an onsite role based in Los Angeles. Regular monthly travel to Los Gatos is expected to support in-person collaboration.What You Will DoLead the creation of an AI-powered Creative Suite, consolidating multimodal AI capabilities to provide a state-of-the-art artist experience.Partner closely with UX/UI design, AI R&D, engineering, user research teams, and creatives to deliver scalable products with production-ready features that artists love.Own the end-to-end product strategy for an AI-assisted experience, from production to post, spanning image and video generation, modification, and timeline-based editing. Ensure creators can transition smoothly from generative exploration to precise, deterministic control.Define and secure interoperability with Netflix platforms and various DCC tools to fit and scale to real-world studio pipelines.Build seamless collaboration features for creative teams, supporting shared projects, versioning, review, and complex production workflows.Lead the product roadmap by identifying high-impact opportunities, prioritizing features, defining success metrics, and supporting end-to-end delivery.Drive rapid iteration through prototyping, experimentation, and close engagement with creators, designers, and researchers, maintaining an "AI-builder" mindset.Partner with peer PMs, Studio Ops, Production Services, and Content leaders to align on requirements, milestones, and slate needs, while driving adoption. What We’re Looking For5+ years of Product Management experience, with a proven track record of building video creation, editing, or production tools for creators or professional teams.Deep domain expertise in AI video generation and modification workflows, and filmmaking techniques using AI-assisted production and post-production techniques.Proven experience delivering products that support both individual creators and enterprise-scale collaborative workflows.Strong product judgment and a builder’s mindset to transform explorationary product features into cohesive, professional-grade products.Excellent communication skills, with the ability to align research, design, and creative practitioners around a unified vision.

title: Manager, Motion Design - Brand Creative Studio
description:
At Netflix, our mission is to entertain the world. Together, we are writing the next episode - pushing the boundaries of storytelling, global fandom and making the unimaginable a reality. We are a dream team obsessed with the uncomfortable excitement of discovering what happens when you merge creativity, intuition and cutting-edge technology. Come be a part of what’s next.Manager, Motion Design, Brand Creative StudioTHE ROLEWe are seeking a Manager, Motion Design to join the Brand Creative Studio. This role is a hands-on motion designer responsible for developing and executing motion systems across a wide range of brand and marketing initiatives, including the Netflix Brand, Netflix House, Product Marketing, and Corporate Communications.This role sits across both Core Design and Production Design, bridging high-level creative development and large-scale execution. The focus is on translating brand strategy, creative direction, and design systems into scalable motion outputs across channels, formats, and markets.You will play a critical role in defining how motion design operates within Brand Creative Studio, contributing to both concept development and high-volume execution. You will partner closely with cross-functional teams and external agencies to ensure motion work is delivered with clarity, craft, and consistency.A strong portfolio demonstrating excellence in motion design across campaigns, systems thinking, and multi-format execution is required.THE TEAMThe Netflix Brand Creative Studio (BCS) is our in-house team of Strategists, Creatives, Designers, Producers, and Program Managers. We are the creative collective behind Netflix’s most high-profile brand initiatives, developing work that builds passionate fandoms and shapes how the world connects with our brand. Our mission is to strengthen the Netflix brand and drive business value by creating culturally relevant marketing that is as entertaining as our shows, films, games, and experiences. Our studio serves as a centralized partner for a wide range of business-critical areas—spanning Global Brand and Brand Partnerships, Ads, Consumer Products, and live experiences like Netflix House. As Netflix continues to expand into new business units, formats, and physical experiences, the volume and complexity of design output across the company continues to grow. To support this scale, BCS is evolving its operating model to include a dedicated production design team focused on speed, systems, and consistency, without compromising craft.RESPONSIBILITIESMotion Design & Creative DevelopmentContribute to the development of motion concepts and creative direction across campaigns, helping define how motion enhances storytelling and brand expression
 Partner with designers and creative leads to integrate motion into early-stage concept development
 Ensure motion solutions are aligned with brand strategy and creative intent across all touchpoints
 Scalable Motion Systems & ProductionTranslate creative direction and brand systems into scalable motion frameworks, templates, and toolkits
 Develop motion systems that can be applied across formats including social, digital, video, DOOH, and experiential environments
 Support high-volume production needs, including lower-funnel assets such as banners, cutdowns, and performance-driven creative
 Cross-Functional Creative SupportServe as a motion design partner to teams across Brand, Netflix House, Product Marketing, Ads, and Corporate Communications
 Ensure motion work is fit-for-purpose across different audiences, channels, and business needs
 Agency & Partner ManagementCollaborate with and provide direction to external motion vendors and agency partners
 Ensure external work aligns with Netflix brand standards, motion systems, and production frameworks
 Support decisions on when work should be executed internally versus externally
 Quality, Craft & Workflow DevelopmentMaintain a high bar for motion design quality across all outputs
 Help define workflows, file structures, and best practices for motion production at scale
 Identify opportunities to improve efficiency through templates, systems, and tooling
 Stay current on motion design trends, tools, and emerging technologies
 QUALIFICATIONS10+ years of experience in motion design within a creative agency, design studio, or scaled in-house brand team
 An exceptional portfolio demonstrating motion design across brand, campaign, and digital work
 Strong foundation in design, with the ability to contribute to broader visual systems beyond motion
 Experience working across a wide range of formats, including social, digital, video, OOH, and experiential
 Proven experience building motion templates, toolkits, or systems that support high-volume production
 Experience managing and directing external vendors and agency partners
 Proficiency in After Effects, Premiere, Cinema 4D, Figma, and Adobe Creative Suite; familiarity with scalable production workflows
 Strong collaboration skills with experience working across cross-functional teams
 Ability to move fluidly between concept development and hands-on execution
 Strong communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills
 Comfortable navigating ambiguity and evolving workflows in a fast-paced environment
 A hands-on designer who can contribute directly to the work while helping shape how motion is executed at scale Generally, our compensation structure consists solely of an annual salary; we do not have bonuses. title: Software Engineer 6 - Ads Account Foundations
description: At Netflix, our mission is to entertain the world. Together, we are writing the next episode - pushing the boundaries of storytelling, global fandom and making the unimaginable a reality. We are a dream team obsessed with the uncomfortable excitement of discovering what happens when you merge creativity, intuition and cutting-edge technology. Come be a part of what’s next.We launched a new ad-supported tier in November 2022 and we are now in the third season of ads.Our new tier allows us to offer our members more choice in how they consume content, attract new members at a lower price point, and create a compelling path for advertisers to reach deeply engaged audiences. We are well into year four of our advertising journey with more exciting things to come!Our TeamOur team is new yet faces the enormous ambition of building highly performant advertising systems and delivering high impact to our business by monetizing our incredible slate of content. As one of the newest entrants in the rapidly growing Connected TV advertising space, we seek to build unique value propositions that help us differentiate from the competition and become a market leader in record time.The Ads Platform Engineering org builds advertising systems and integrations that power ad delivery using our world-class content delivery ecosystem. We use a number of Netflix investments and innovations to power our ads, a unique mix of client and server-side ad insertions, state-of-the-art content delivery systems, ad encoding recipes, content understanding and metadata, and more. We deliver ads in a manner that's thoughtful of our members' viewing experience and drive great outcomes for advertisers.The Account Foundations team owns the accounts domain for Netflix Ads, i.e the system that defines who our advertisers are, what they're eligible for, and how they transact. It sounds like table stakes, but we don't have a lot of the moving pieces in place yet. Today, account data is scattered across systems, hierarchies are incomplete, and too much of what should be automated is still done manually. Every team building on top of advertiser data, such as sales workflows, media planning, programmatic integrations, and packaging, runs into these gaps. Our job is to fix that from the ground up and make sure it doesn't become a problem again, as the business scales.We're working across four areas:Core Accounts: We're rebuilding the account data model that can actually represent how advertisers and agencies are structured in the real world, representing HoldCo hierarchies, brand relationships, and multi-tier agency structures with strong governance being enforced. This also enables the creation of externalized APIs, allowing advertisers and agencies to view and manage their accounts as we move into a world of agenctic buying.Account Intelligence: We're building the enrichment layer to identify who our advertisers are, where they spend their advertising budget, how they're structured, and whether our internal data matches reality. Account Cohort and Verification Automation: Cohort assignment and identity verification are both manual today and both scale linearly with advertiser growth. Handling these problems at scale in an automated manner will be one of the key drivers of our long term success.Spend Attribution: We are building an automated attribution engine to accurately map programmatic spend from DSPs and map it with the corresponding advertisers, agencies, and ad accounts across supply channels.The work is foundational, which means it's both high leverage and high stakes, as well as a truly zero-to-one space. The architectural decisions we make now will be load-bearing for every team in Netflix Ads for years. We are responsible for tracking and managing all music used across Netflix content globally, ensuring accurate cue sheet reporting and compliance with CMO obligations. We partner closely with productions, internal stakeholders, and our publishing administrator to support cue sheet creation and delivery and enable timely royalty distribution. Our team maintains high-quality music data while overseeing the administration and continued growth of Netflix’s music catalog.THE ROLE In this unique and exciting role, you will be responsible for vital components of our music cue sheet and music administration processes, and will have visibility into all facets of our music business.  As a cue sheet expert, you know the exact process of using music in a production and understand the significance of synchronization licenses, publishing splits, IPI numbers, and PRO information.  You have mastered the art of cue sheet preparation and review, and in your view, there is no cue sheet data that is too small or insignificant.  You know how to review a composer agreement and synchronization license for pertinent cue sheet information and are effective in aligning music supervisors, music editors, and other production personnel to deliver proper cue sheet data. Your thorough understanding of why cue sheets matter results in a keen attention to detail and accuracy that you impart to everyone involved in the music production process. You are familiar and have achieved mastery of at least one of the many cue sheet tools currently available. data for rights.Participating in other projects specific to Netflix's music needs.About You:You will have at least 3-5 years of experience with music cue sheets, preferably in the music department at
